name: Cuckoo Filter

x.invariant: each element maps to two candidate buckets; a fingerprint lives in one of them; buckets hold a small number of fingerprint slots.
x.memoryOverhead: A few bits per element above the information-theoretic bound; competitive with Bloom at low false-positive rates and supports deletion.
x.locality: Two bucket probes per query; cache-friendlier than a Bloom filter with many hash probes.
x.mutationModel: Mutable; insertion can fail (cascade of relocations) when load factor is too high → resize/rebuild.
x.concurrency: Not thread-safe by default; relocation makes lock-free variants hard.

def: A compact approximate-membership structure that, unlike a Bloom filter, supports deletion and gives better locality at low false-positive rates.
problem: Test set membership in far less space than the keys, while still being able to remove items.
mechanism: Stores a short fingerprint of each key in one of two candidate buckets (cuckoo hashing). On insertion conflict it evicts and relocates existing fingerprints; lookup probes both buckets.
benefits: Lower space than Bloom at low FP rates
limits: Insertion can fail at high load → must resize
Duplicate inserts limited by slot count
failures: False positives still possible by design
whenNot: Deletion never needed and Bloom is simpler
Exact membership required (use a hash set)
myths: "No false positives" — it is still probabilistic, just deletable
